tests.scope: Exercise the read-only user-attachment methods shipped in issue #39 against live SANDBOX. Calls list_user_attachments(${existing_user_primary_id}) to confirm the GET /users/{user_id}/attachments endpoint round-trips and returns a List[Dict] envelope successfully unwrapped from Alma's response shape. If the returned list is non-empty, picks the first attachment's id and calls get_user_attachment(${existing_user_primary_id}, <attachment_id>) to confirm the single-attachment GET round-trips and returns a non-empty Dict[str, Any] containing an 'id' key. If the list is empty (the SANDBOX user has no attachments), the get-step is skipped and only the list-shape assertion is verified — the test is still considered pass because the list endpoint itself responded correctly. No state is mutated. Covers the read half of issue #39's 'list / get without expand' integration coverage; the upload+read-back round-trip is exercised by t-39-3.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma user_primary_id in SANDBOX that the operator has authorized for read-only and write smoke testing. Used by t-39-1 (read-only list/get), t-39-3 (live attachment upload — leaves a permanent artifact, see that test's scope), t-44-1 (read-only fee list/get), and t-45-1 (read-only deposit list). The user is not deleted or otherwise destructively mutated; t-39-3 attaches a small probe HTML file that the operator must manually delete via the Alma UI after the run. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real user_primary_id into this file.

pythonCalls: from almaapitk.domains.users import Users
users = Users(client)
attachments = users.list_user_attachments('${existing_user_primary_id}')

first_attachment = None
attachment_detail = None
if isinstance(attachments, list) and len(attachments) > 0:
    first_attachment = attachments[0]
    attachment_id = first_attachment.get('id') if isinstance(first_attachment, dict) else None
    if attachment_id:
        attachment_detail = users.get_user_attachment('${existing_user_primary_id}', str(attachment_id))
passCriteria: first_attachment is None or (isinstance(attachment_detail, dict) and 'id' in attachment_detail)

Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated. Covers the AlmaValidationError half of issue #39's 'unit tests cover ... upload with missing file' AC and the input-validation contract for the public API surface.

tests.scope: Operator-authorized live upload_user_attachment round-trip against the supplied SANDBOX user ${existing_user_primary_id}. Generates synthetic test content in-test (a tiny HTML file with a UUID-suffixed marker so concurrent runs do not collide and so the operator can identify the artifact in the Alma UI), writes it to a NamedTemporaryFile, then calls upload_user_attachment with attachment_data of {'type': 'GENERAL', 'note': 'AlmaAPITK regression-smoke probe'}. Asserts the returned AlmaResponse reports success and exposes an 'id' on its data payload, then calls list_user_attachments and asserts the list contains a dict whose id matches the upload response's id AND whose file_name contains 'almaapitk' or matches the temp filename basename. CRITICAL OPERATOR CLEANUP REQUIRED: Alma exposes NO DELETE endpoint for user attachments (verified live 2026-05-08). Each test run accumulates ONE additional attachment on the supplied user, which the operator MUST manually delete via the Alma staff UI after the run. There is no try/finally cleanup block because no API call exists to clean up. The test prints the attachment id, user id, and synthetic file_name at runtime so the regression-smoke output makes the cleanup target visible to the operator. The probe payload is intentionally tiny and human-recognizable ('AlmaAPITK probe attachment - safe to delete - <UUID>') to make manual deletion safe and unambiguous. Covers issue #39 AC about a SANDBOX integration test that round-trips an attachment (upload then list-and-verify); the GET-with-expand variant is covered by unit tests (mocked HTTP) only — see unmappable[].

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma user_primary_id in SANDBOX that the operator has authorized for read-only and write smoke testing. Used by t-39-1 (read-only list/get), t-39-3 (live attachment upload — leaves a permanent artifact, see that test's scope), t-44-1 (read-only fee list/get), and t-45-1 (read-only deposit list). The user is not deleted or otherwise destructively mutated; t-39-3 attaches a small probe HTML file that the operator must manually delete via the Alma UI after the run. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real user_primary_id into this file.

pythonCalls: import os
import uuid
from tempfile import NamedTemporaryFile
from almaapitk.domains.users import Users
users = Users(client)
marker = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
html_payload = f'<!-- AlmaAPITK probe attachment - safe to delete - {marker} -->\n<html><body>probe-{marker}</body></html>\n'

tmp = NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix=f'_almaapitk_probe_{marker}.html', delete=False, encoding='utf-8')
try:
    tmp.write(html_payload)
    tmp.close()
    file_path = tmp.name
    file_basename = os.path.basename(file_path)
    upload_response = users.upload_user_attachment(
        '${existing_user_primary_id}',
        file_path,
        attachment_data={'type': 'GENERAL', 'note': 'AlmaAPITK regression-smoke probe'},
    )
    upload_data = getattr(upload_response, 'data', None) if upload_response is not None else None
    upload_id = upload_data.get('id') if isinstance(upload_data, dict) else None
    print(f'[t-39-3] uploaded attachment id={upload_id} user_id=${existing_user_primary_id} file_basename={file_basename} -- OPERATOR MUST MANUALLY DELETE VIA ALMA UI (no DELETE endpoint exists)')
    listing = users.list_user_attachments('${existing_user_primary_id}')
    matched = None
    if isinstance(listing, list) and upload_id is not None:
        for entry in listing:
            if not isinstance(entry, dict):
                continue
            entry_id = entry.get('id')
            entry_file_name = entry.get('file_name') or ''
            if str(entry_id) == str(upload_id):
                if 'almaapitk' in str(entry_file_name).lower() or file_basename in str(entry_file_name):
                    matched = entry
                    break
finally:
    try:
        os.unlink(tmp.name)
    except OSError:
        pass
passCriteria: upload_response is not None and getattr(upload_response, 'success', False) is True
isinstance(upload_data, dict) and 'id' in upload_data and upload_id is not None
isinstance(listing, list) and matched is not None

unmappable.ac: All three endpoints exposed with type hints and Google-style docstrings.
unmappable.reason: The 'with type hints and Google-style docstrings' facet is a static-source invariant that cannot be observed via SANDBOX behaviour — it is verified by code review of the chunk diff and (transitively) by tests/test_public_api_contract.py. The 'methods exist on the existing Users class' facet IS observable at runtime (t-39-1 only succeeds if list_user_attachments/get_user_attachment are importable and bound on Users; upload_user_attachment is exercised end-to-end by t-39-3), so the AC is partially mapped to t-39-1 / t-39-3; this entry documents the residual static-only facet.

The expand-parameter facet — specifically the 'get with expand=content' / 'get with expand=content_no_encoding' variants — is intentionally NOT exercised against SANDBOX. The base get_user_attachment(user_id, attachment_id) call without expand IS exercised by t-39-1 when the user has at least one existing attachment, which proves the path-parameter binding and the no-expand happy path. The expand=content / expand=content_no_encoding variants are wire-shape concerns (query-string assembly, response decoding) covered by the chunk's unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_users.py with mocked HTTP. Adding a SANDBOX expand-content fetch would require a known attachment with a verified inline payload, which the operator has not designated; the unit-test coverage is sufficient.

unmappable.ac: `upload_user_attachment` body shape matches what the developer-network
unmappable.reason: Partially mapped to t-39-3 — the live upload only succeeds end-to-end if the wrapper's serialized body shape (multipart vs JSON+base64 Attachment object) is what Alma actually accepts, so t-39-3 is the strongest possible behavioural verification. The 'matches what the developer-network docs require' facet is otherwise a doc-conformance invariant verified by code review of the chunk diff against the developer-network 'Create Attachment' page, plus the unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_users.py that pin the exact body shape. SANDBOX cannot distinguish between two body shapes that both happen to be accepted; the doc-conformance facet is a residual static-only check.

unmappable.ac: Unit tests (mocked HTTP) cover: list, get without expand, get with
unmappable.reason: The 'unit tests (mocked HTTP)' facet is a local pytest-suite deliverable (tests/unit/domains/test_users.py) verified pre-merge by the test runner, not by Alma SANDBOX behaviour. The 'integration test against SANDBOX' facet for the list+get+upload paths IS exercised by t-39-1 and t-39-3; this entry documents the unit-test residual.

unmappable.ac: No DELETE method is implemented (Alma does not document one).
unmappable.reason: Negative-existence invariant on src/almaapitk/domains/users.py — the AC asserts that NO delete_user_attachment method is implemented. Cannot be verified via SANDBOX (no endpoint exists to call). Verified by code review of the chunk diff and the public-API contract test (which would detect a new public symbol). Operator note: this is precisely WHY t-39-3 has no try/finally cleanup — Alma exposes no DELETE endpoint for user attachments, so each run accumulates one attachment that the operator must delete manually via the Alma staff UI.

Observable only by inspecting the source (no `print(` tokens in the new methods, file contents not passed into log records) and by reviewing the `logs/` directory after the chunk runs. Verified by code review of the chunk diff.

tests.scope: Confirm every input-validation guard added in issue #44 raises AlmaValidationError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued, including the audit-corrected guards: pay_all_user_fees rejects non-numeric / non-'ALL' amount strings; waive_user_fee requires non-empty reason; dispute_user_fee does NOT require reason. Exercises: list_user_fees / get_user_fee guards on user_id and fee_id (empty/whitespace/None/non-string); create_user_fee on empty/None user_id and on missing/non-dict fee_data; pay_all_user_fees on empty/None user_id and on amount='abc' (audit-flagged: must reject non-numeric, non-'ALL'); pay_user_fee on empty/None user_id, empty/None fee_id, and missing amount; waive_user_fee on empty/None reason (audit-flagged: required); dispute_user_fee called WITHOUT reason and asserted to NOT raise AlmaValidationError on the reason guard (audit-corrected: optional); restore_user_fee on empty/None user_id and fee_id. Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated. Covers the AlmaValidationError half of issue #44 AC 'Errors raise AlmaValidationError (input) or AlmaAPIError / subclass (API).' and the audit-corrected signature contracts.

unmappable.reason: The validation half (audit-corrected: pay_all_user_fees rejects non-numeric, non-'ALL' amount values) is mapped to t-44-2. The wire-shape facet (the actual op=pay & amount=... & method=... query string posted to /fees/all) is NOT exercised against SANDBOX because no pre-existing fee fixture is available on the supplied test user, and creating a fee in SANDBOX to exercise the bulk-pay endpoint would accumulate state with no automated cleanup (Alma's fee restore op is not a true delete). Unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_users.py with mocked HTTP cover the exact wire shape including the audit-flagged op=pay + amount=ALL + method query params; this entry documents the deliberate gap in SANDBOX coverage.

Same reason as pay_all_user_fees above: validation guards are exercised by t-44-2, but the live POST .../fees/{fee_id}?op=pay&amount=...&method=... is NOT exercised against SANDBOX because no pre-existing fee fixture is available on the supplied test user, and creating a fee solely to pay it would accumulate state. Unit tests cover the wire shape with mocked HTTP.

The validation half (waive_user_fee requires non-empty reason — audit-corrected to be the ONLY op that requires reason) is mapped to t-44-2. The wire-shape facet (POST .../fees/{fee_id}?op=waive&reason=...) is NOT exercised against SANDBOX for the same reason as the pay ops: no pre-existing fee fixture, and creating then waiving a fee would accumulate state. Unit tests cover the op=waive wire shape with mocked HTTP.

The validation half (dispute_user_fee does NOT raise AlmaValidationError when reason is omitted — audit-corrected: dispute does NOT require reason, unlike waive) is mapped to t-44-2 via the dispute_no_reason_validation_raised assertion. The wire-shape facet (POST .../fees/{fee_id}?op=dispute) is NOT exercised against SANDBOX for the same reason as the other write ops: no pre-existing fee fixture, and creating then disputing a fee would accumulate state. Unit tests cover the op=dispute wire shape with mocked HTTP, including the audit-corrected absence of a required reason param.

Validation guards (empty/None user_id, empty/None fee_id) are mapped to t-44-2. The live POST .../fees/{fee_id}?op=restore is NOT exercised against SANDBOX for the same reason as the other write ops: no pre-existing fee fixture in a disputed/waived state available, and the operator declined to authorize a create→dispute→restore flow that would leave state visible in the SANDBOX user's account. Unit tests cover the op=restore wire shape with mocked HTTP.

unmappable.ac: No method silently injects `method='CASH'` outside payment ops.
unmappable.reason: Negative-behaviour invariant on the wrapper. The audit flagged that 'method' should NOT default into waive/dispute/restore query strings; only pay_all_user_fees and pay_user_fee should accept and forward 'method'. Cannot be observed via SANDBOX because Alma silently ignores extra query params on non-payment ops. Verified by code review of the chunk diff (no method= kwarg on waive/dispute/restore signatures) and by tests/unit/domains/test_users.py which pin the exact query strings issued by waive/dispute/restore — no method= present.

unmappable.ac: Unit tests cover each of the 4 `op` values, plus the `pay_all` shape.
unmappable.reason: The 'unit tests (mocked HTTP)' facet is a local pytest-suite deliverable (tests/unit/domains/test_users.py) verified pre-merge by the test runner, not by Alma SANDBOX behaviour. The validation guard half IS exercised by t-44-2; the read happy-path is exercised by t-44-1; this entry documents the unit-test residual.

unmappable.ac: At least one SANDBOX integration test for each `op`.
unmappable.reason: DELIBERATE SCOPE LIMIT: the operator has not authorized live execution of any write op (create / pay_all / pay / waive / dispute / restore) against SANDBOX. Reason: no pre-existing fee fixture is available on the supplied test user, and creating a fee solely to exercise an op would accumulate state with no automated cleanup (Alma's fee 'restore' is not a delete; it just reverts a waive/dispute, and a freshly-created fee remains as ACTIVE). Unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_users.py with mocked HTTP cover the wire shape for all four op values plus pay_all, including the audit-flagged param requirements. This is the single biggest deliberate gap in the SANDBOX coverage for this chunk and is documented here for transparency.

Logging-call invariant on the source. SANDBOX returns identical bytes regardless of whether the wrapper logs or prints. Observable only by inspecting the source (no `print(` tokens in the new methods) and by reviewing the `logs/` directory after the chunk runs. Verified by code review of the chunk diff.

Local-suite invariant. Both are run by the operator (and CI) in the local environment; no Alma SANDBOX call would observe their outcome. Verified pre-merge by `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and `poetry run pytest tests/test_public_api_contract.py -v`, not by SANDBOX behaviour.

unmappable.ac: All listed methods implemented on the existing domain class with type hints and docstrings.
unmappable.reason: The 'with type hints and docstrings' facet is a static-source invariant that cannot be observed via SANDBOX behaviour — it is verified by code review of the chunk diff and (transitively) by tests/test_public_api_contract.py. The 'methods exist on the existing Users class' facet IS partially observable at runtime: t-45-1 only succeeds if list_user_deposits is importable and bound on Users, and t-45-2 only succeeds if create_user_deposit, get_user_deposit, and perform_user_deposit_action are importable and bound on Users (because the validation guards live on those methods). So the AC is partially mapped to t-45-1 / t-45-2; this entry documents the residual static-only facet.

unmappable.ac: Each method has unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) and at least one integration test against SANDBOX.
unmappable.reason: The 'unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP)' facet is a local pytest-suite deliverable (tests/unit/domains/test_users.py) verified pre-merge by the test runner, not by Alma SANDBOX behaviour. The 'at least one integration test against SANDBOX' facet IS exercised by t-45-1 for list_user_deposits. The integration-test facet for create_user_deposit, get_user_deposit, and perform_user_deposit_action is intentionally NOT exercised against SANDBOX. Reason: no pre-existing deposit fixture is available on the supplied test user, and creating a deposit solely to exercise these endpoints would accumulate state with no automated cleanup (Alma's deposit perform-action ops are state transitions, not deletes — a freshly-created deposit remains in the user's account regardless of what subsequent ops are applied). Unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_users.py with mocked HTTP cover the wire shape for create / get / perform-action; this entry documents both the unit-test residual AND the deliberate gap in SANDBOX coverage for the write paths.

The input-validation half (AlmaValidationError on bad inputs) is mapped to t-45-2. The API-error half (AlmaAPIError/subclass on real Alma failures) is harder to provoke deterministically against SANDBOX — most failure paths (404 on a non-existent deposit_id, 401 on bad auth, 400 on malformed deposit_data) are environment-dependent and would flake. Existing unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_users.py cover the API-error path with mocked HTTP; this entry documents that residual unit-only facet.

Logging-call invariant on the source. SANDBOX returns identical bytes regardless of whether the wrapper logs or prints. Observable only by inspecting the source (no `print(` tokens in the new methods) and by reviewing the `logs/` directory after the chunk runs. Verified by code review of the chunk diff.

Local-suite invariant. Both are run by the operator (and CI) in the local environment; no Alma SANDBOX call would observe their outcome. Verified pre-merge by `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and `poetry run pytest tests/test_public_api_contract.py -v`, not by SANDBOX behaviour.
